channel.title: Feeds Student Journalism and feeds about Journalism feeds

item.title: Third walk-off wins UCLA baseball Big Ten championship against Oregon in extras
item.description: This post was updated May 24 at 5:41 p.m. The comeback kids did it again.  No. 1 seed UCLA (51-6, 28-2 Big Ten) bested No. 3 seed Oregon (40-16, 20-10) 3-2 in 11 innings to win the Big Ten championship, achieving its third walk-off victory of the conference tournament in just as many days.  With the bases loaded and the score tied 2-2 in the bottom of the 11th, Oregon right-hander Devin Bell sailed a fastball high and tight to junior infielder Phoenix Call, which the home plate umpire ruled as a hit-by-pitch.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 24 at 5:41 p.m._ The comeback kids did it again. No. 1 seed UCLA (51-6, 28-2 Big Ten) bested No. 3 seed Oregon (40-16, 20-10) 3-2 in 11 innings to win the Big Ten championship, achieving its third walk-off victory of the conference tournament in just as many days. With the bases loaded and the score tied 2-2 in the bottom of the 11th, Oregon right-hander Devin Bell sailed a fastball high and tight to junior infielder Phoenix Call, which the home plate umpire ruled as a hit-by-pitch.

item.title: Opinion: Care for women’s sports is just some curiosity and a Google search away
item.description: April 5, 2026, was the proudest I have ever felt as a Bruin. Our women’s basketball team beat the South Carolina Gamecocks 79-51 in the NCAA championship.
item.source:markdown: April 5, 2026, was the proudest I have ever felt as a Bruin. Our women’s basketball team beat the South Carolina Gamecocks 79-51 in the NCAA championship.

item.title: Opinion: Bruin Walk’s aggressive advertising culture pushes students away, needs reform
item.description: A swarm of bodies is blocking your way, begging for attention. They keep talking to you, despite your disinterest. They even ask for your autograph. You could be an A-list celebrity – or a UCLA student walking down Bruin Walk.
item.source:markdown: A swarm of bodies is blocking your way, begging for attention. They keep talking to you, despite your disinterest. They even ask for your autograph. You could be an A-list celebrity – or a UCLA student walking down Bruin Walk.

item.title: Interim dean of Luskin School of Public Affairs soon to be permanently instated
item.description: A distinguished professor in urban planning will become the permanent dean of the UCLA Luskin School of Public Affairs on June 1. Anastasia Loukaitou-Sideris, who has worked at UCLA for 35 years and served as the interim dean since January 2023, said her appointment represents a continuation of initiatives she began as interim dean – including new academic programs and expanded student support.
item.source:markdown: A distinguished professor in urban planning will become the permanent dean of the UCLA Luskin School of Public Affairs on June 1. Anastasia Loukaitou-Sideris, who has worked at UCLA for 35 years and served as the interim dean since January 2023, said her appointment represents a continuation of initiatives she began as interim dean – including new academic programs and expanded student support.

item.title: Winner takes all: The best, this ‘Star Wars’ movie is. After nearly 50 years, which film is on top?
item.description: “Star Wars” is known for its galaxywide fame and success. Since its debut in 1977, the “Star Wars” franchise has expanded far beyond a single trilogy, growing into one of the most influential sci-fi sagas in cinematic history.
item.source:markdown: “Star Wars” is known for its galaxywide fame and success. Since its debut in 1977, the “Star Wars” franchise has expanded far beyond a single trilogy, growing into one of the most influential sci-fi sagas in cinematic history.

item.title: UCLA softball scores 10-run win over UCF, advances to Women’s College World Series
item.description: This post was updated May 23 at 11:33 p.m. No one has more home runs – with 200 – in a single season.  Now, no one has more runs in a single season – with 651 – than the Oklahoma City-bound Bruins.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 23 at 11:33 p.m._ No one has more home runs – with 200 – in a single season. Now, no one has more runs in a single season – with 651 – than the Oklahoma City-bound Bruins.

item.title: 2 days, 2 walk-offs: Mulivai Levu pushes UCLA baseball past USC in 7-5 victory
item.description: This post was updated May 23 at 5:45 p.m. On back-to-back days at the Big Ten tournament, junior first baseman Mulivai Levu was the walk-off hero.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 23 at 5:45 p.m._ On back-to-back days at the Big Ten tournament, junior first baseman Mulivai Levu was the walk-off hero.

item.title: Gallery: Following 2026 Winter Olympics, Stars on Tour features world champion skaters
item.description: World-class skaters took to the ice at the Honda Center in Anaheim as part of the Stars on Ice Tour on Saturday night. The event featured group and solo performances by Olympic and world champion skaters, including Alysa Liu and Amber Glen.
item.source:markdown: World-class skaters took to the ice at the Honda Center in Anaheim as part of the Stars on Ice Tour on Saturday night. The event featured group and solo performances by Olympic and world champion skaters, including Alysa Liu and Amber Glen.

item.title: Concert review: Student Committee for the Arts hosts next dream-pop icon at annual Royce Hall show
item.description: This post was updated May 22 at 11:07 p.m. Before, many attendees may not have known exactly who they were watching this evening. After, it would be hard to forget.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 22 at 11:07 p.m._ Before, many attendees may not have known exactly who they were watching this evening. After, it would be hard to forget.

item.title: UCLA baseball defeats Purdue in walk-off fashion, advances to Big Ten semifinal
item.source:markdown: Nine hours and 20 minutes after the originally posted game time, junior first baseman Mulivai Levu put a bow on the never-ending day. The Bruins had to wait an eternity for a celebration, but it was well worth it. No.

item.title: UCLA softball secures run-rule victory over UCF in 1st game of NCAA Super Regional
item.description: The story of the Bruins’ season has been home runs and high-scoring games. But with a 3-1 lead entering the bottom of the fifth inning, this game looked to be an exception.
item.source:markdown: The story of the Bruins’ season has been home runs and high-scoring games. But with a 3-1 lead entering the bottom of the fifth inning, this game looked to be an exception.

item.title: 5 candidates are running in primary to represent California Assembly District 51
item.description: Two Democrats – including an incumbent – and a Republican retired businessman are among the candidates vying to represent UCLA and its surrounding areas in the state assembly.
item.source:markdown: Two Democrats – including an incumbent – and a Republican retired businessman are among the candidates vying to represent UCLA and its surrounding areas in the state assembly.

item.title: UCLA historians preserve documents, photos in ‘Power from the Past’ online archive
item.description: UCLA historians launched a revamped digital labor history archive in April, preserving materials from movements led by Southern California unions. The Memory Work Research Initiative at UCLA – which the Institute for Research on Labor and Employment oversees – digitized documents, images and flyers on an online archive called Power from the Past.
item.source:markdown: UCLA historians launched a revamped digital labor history archive in April, preserving materials from movements led by Southern California unions. The Memory Work Research Initiative at UCLA – which the Institute for Research on Labor and Employment oversees – digitized documents, images and flyers on an online archive called Power from the Past.

item.title: How did sports betting become so normalized among college students?
item.description: Americans were estimated to have legally bet more than $3.3 billion on the NCAA March Madness tournament this year. Sports betting has gained traction among young people in recent years.
item.source:markdown: Americans were estimated to have legally bet more than $3.3 billion on the NCAA March Madness tournament this year. Sports betting has gained traction among young people in recent years.

item.title: UCLA softball Los Angeles Super Regional predictions
item.description: No. 8 seed UCLA softball (50-8, 20-4 Big Ten) will face UCF (41-17-1, 14-9-1 Big 12) in the Los Angeles Super Regional from Friday to Sunday.
item.source:markdown: No. 8 seed UCLA softball (50-8, 20-4 Big Ten) will face UCF (41-17-1, 14-9-1 Big 12) in the Los Angeles Super Regional from Friday to Sunday.

item.title: UCLA softball prepares for weekend Los Angeles super regional
item.source:markdown:
“War of attrition, war of wills.”
Columnist George Vecsey’s infamous quote describes the postseason like no other – after a season of triumphs and disappointments, all is for naught without composure, character and rising up to the challenge on the largest stage.

item.title: UCLA baseball Big Ten tournament predictions 2026
item.description: No. 1 seed UCLA baseball (48-6, 28-2 Big Ten) has officially concluded its regular season and will head to the Big Ten tournament in Omaha after receiving a bye to the single-elimination stage.
item.source:markdown: No. 1 seed UCLA baseball (48-6, 28-2 Big Ten) has officially concluded its regular season and will head to the Big Ten tournament in Omaha after receiving a bye to the single-elimination stage.

item.title: Five things: What could go wrong for UCLA baseball? Well, here are 5 options
item.description: No. 1 UCLA baseball (48-6, 28-2 Big Ten) became the first team to ever spend every week of the regular season at the very top of the top 25.
item.source:markdown: No. 1 UCLA baseball (48-6, 28-2 Big Ten) became the first team to ever spend every week of the regular season at the very top of the top 25.

item.title: AFSCME Local 3299 employees vote to ratify contract
item.description: This post was updated May 22 at 12:29 p.m. A union that represents more than 40,000 workers – and that has been in negotiations with the UC for more than two years – voted to ratify a contract with the University on Friday.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 22 at 12:29 p.m._ A union that represents more than 40,000 workers – and that has been in negotiations with the UC for more than two years – voted to ratify a contract with the University on Friday.

item.title: In Plain Sight: UCLA Marina Aquatic Center staff provides space for recreation, water sports
item.description: This post was updated May 22 at 1:54 a.m. About 10 miles away from campus, staff at UCLA’s waterfront recreation center are providing a natural escape for students and veterans.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 22 at 1:54 a.m._ About 10 miles away from campus, staff at UCLA’s waterfront recreation center are providing a natural escape for students and veterans.

item.title: Professor Catherine Sugar will be 2027-28 chair of Academic Senate
item.description: A professor with appointments in three UCLA departments will serve as the 2027-28 chair of the Academic Senate. Catherine Sugar, who will begin her term as chair Sept.
item.source:markdown: A professor with appointments in three UCLA departments will serve as the 2027-28 chair of the Academic Senate. Catherine Sugar, who will begin her term as chair Sept.

item.title: Daily Bruin Print Issue – May 22, 2026
item.description: The Daily Bruin publishes print issues every Monday, Wednesday and Friday during the school year. You can view the online version of The Bruin here. ﻿
item.source:markdown: The Daily Bruin publishes print issues every Monday, Wednesday and Friday during the school year. You can view the online version of The Bruin here. ﻿

item.title: This costume designer dressed ‘Dune.’ She’s teaching UCLA students her craft.
item.description: Jacqueline West drapes a new vision for world-building in a classroom where students stitch sandstorms from silk. The Academy Award nominee is the latest David C.
item.source:markdown: Jacqueline West drapes a new vision for world-building in a classroom where students stitch sandstorms from silk. The Academy Award nominee is the latest David C.

item.title: Opinion: Police on campus surveil, not support, students
item.description: This post was updated May 21 at 8:46 p.m. UCLA students are no strangers to surveillance. One stroll down Bruin Walk is all the proof you need.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 21 at 8:46 p.m._ UCLA students are no strangers to surveillance. One stroll down Bruin Walk is all the proof you need.

item.title: As goes CBS Radio News, so goes the idea that news media should serve the public interest
item.description: When CBS Radio News goes silent on May 22, 2026, Americans will lose access to news programming they’ve tuned into from their living rooms, kitchens and cars for nearly a century. The once-bipartisan idea that the nation’s media should exist to serve democracy continues to fade with it, too. As a media historian, I think...
item.source:markdown: When CBS Radio News goes silent on May 22, 2026, Americans will lose access to news programming they’ve tuned into from their living rooms, kitchens and cars for nearly a century. The once-bipartisan idea that the nation’s media should exist to serve democracy continues to fade with it, too. As a media historian, I think...

item.title: More than 340 local news outlets are limiting the Internet Archive’s access to their journalism
item.description: In January, Nieman Lab broke the story that major news publishers — including The New York Times, The Guardian, and USA Today Co. — had started blocking the Internet Archive due to concerns that AI companies might scrape the nonprofit’s repositories for training data. No news publisher has confirmed to Nieman Lab that an AI...
item.source:markdown: In January, Nieman Lab broke the story that major news publishers — including The New York Times, The Guardian, and USA Today Co. — had started blocking the Internet Archive due to concerns that AI companies might scrape the nonprofit’s repositories for training data. No news publisher has confirmed to Nieman Lab that an AI...

item.title: James Murdoch buys up half of Vox Media, grabbing New York and podcasts, but leaving The Verge and SB Nation
item.description: This is May 2026 in digital media: Arguably the two most prominent digital media startups of the 2010s are both being sold — one to the former host of NBC reality show “Real People” (1979-84) and one to the primary inspiration for Kendall Roy (2018-23). On May 11, it was standup-comic-turned-media-mogul Byron Allen acquiring a...
item.source:markdown: This is May 2026 in digital media: Arguably the two most prominent digital media startups of the 2010s are both being sold — one to the former host of NBC reality show “Real People” (1979-84) and one to the primary inspiration for Kendall Roy (2018-23). On May 11, it was standup-comic-turned-media-mogul Byron Allen acquiring a...

item.title: Tech journalist Joanna Stern on leaving the Wall Street Journal and moving on to New Things
item.description: Joanna Stern is no stranger to new things. It’s part of the job: Stern began working as a technology journalist in 2007, the year Apple launched the first iPhone, and has covered the shifts in the industry through the rise of smartphones, the mobile internet, and AI. Along the way, she won an Emmy and...
item.source:markdown: Joanna Stern is no stranger to new things. It’s part of the job: Stern began working as a technology journalist in 2007, the year Apple launched the first iPhone, and has covered the shifts in the industry through the rise of smartphones, the mobile internet, and AI. Along the way, she won an Emmy and...

item.title: NPR may be flush with gifts to transform its tech, but it still has to cut jobs
item.description: Last month, NPR announced two private gifts totaling $113 million — among the largest donations it’s received in its history. The $80 million donation, from philanthropist Connie Ballmer, is specifically for “ensuring NPR transforms its technology to meet the needs and serve the interests of public media audiences on whatever platforms or devices they may...
item.source:markdown: Last month, NPR announced two private gifts totaling $113 million — among the largest donations it’s received in its history. The $80 million donation, from philanthropist Connie Ballmer, is specifically for “ensuring NPR transforms its technology to meet the needs and serve the interests of public media audiences on whatever platforms or devices they may...

item.title: Sam Altman backs “micropayment” model for AI agents to compensate publishers
item.description: Late last month, Sam Altman sat down with Nicholas Thompson, the CEO of The Atlantic, for a podcast episode of “The Most Interesting Thing in AI.” The show is produced by Re:think, the publication’s marketing and branded content studio. One clip has been making the rounds on social media the past couple days. In a...
item.source:markdown: Late last month, Sam Altman sat down with Nicholas Thompson, the CEO of The Atlantic, for a podcast episode of “The Most Interesting Thing in AI.” The show is produced by Re:think, the publication’s marketing and branded content studio. One clip has been making the rounds on social media the past couple days. In a...

item.title: Here’s a new database for local news research, from Syracuse University and Rebuild Local News
item.description: If you’re trying to get a handle on evidence from academic research about the state of local news, it’s hard to know where to start. The research is scattered — across disciplines from political science to economics to computer science; across universities; across paywalled journals. To some extent, it’s part of the academic job description...
item.source:markdown: If you’re trying to get a handle on evidence from academic research about the state of local news, it’s hard to know where to start. The research is scattered — across disciplines from political science to economics to computer science; across universities; across paywalled journals. To some extent, it’s part of the academic job description...

item.title: Creator journalism is the most disruptive shift the news industry has seen, ex-BBC News head says
item.description: If broadcasters want to rebuild trust and remain relevant, they must “liberate their talent” and let their journalists act more like independent creators, Deborah Turness said in a speech in London this week. “I believe the established media hasn’t confronted the hard truth that this revolution isn’t just about consumers moving to different platforms,” Turness,...
item.source:markdown: If broadcasters want to rebuild trust and remain relevant, they must “liberate their talent” and let their journalists act more like independent creators, Deborah Turness said in a speech in London this week. “I believe the established media hasn’t confronted the hard truth that this revolution isn’t just about consumers moving to different platforms,” Turness,...

item.title: Local stories by nonprofit college media resonated nationally at the start of 2026
item.description: At Nieman Lab, we’ve long covered the impact of student-run news outlets filling information gaps in their communities. Recent data from SimilarWeb shows just how far their work reaches. In February, three Southern college media outlets — The Duke Chronicle (Duke University), The Daily Tar Heel (University of North Carolina — Chapel Hill), and the...
item.source:markdown: At Nieman Lab, we’ve long covered the impact of student-run news outlets filling information gaps in their communities. Recent data from SimilarWeb shows just how far their work reaches. In February, three Southern college media outlets — The Duke Chronicle (Duke University), The Daily Tar Heel (University of North Carolina — Chapel Hill), and the...

item.title: When ICE came to Minneapolis, readers turned to The Minnesota Star Tribune’s free live blog
item.description: When Minneapolis became ground zero for the Trump administration’s unprecedented federal immigration crackdown, its local news institutions were, once again, on the front lines of a global story. And just as Minnesota’s nonprofits and public radio station punched above their audience weight with their reporting, so too did its local newspapers. The flagship Minnesota Star...
item.source:markdown: When Minneapolis became ground zero for the Trump administration’s unprecedented federal immigration crackdown, its local news institutions were, once again, on the front lines of a global story. And just as Minnesota’s nonprofits and public radio station punched above their audience weight with their reporting, so too did its local newspapers. The flagship Minnesota Star...

item.title: OPINION: My great-grandparents survived genocide before 1915
item.description: History marks 1915 as the beginning of the Armenian Genocide, but my ancestors knew its shadow long before the world gave it a date. My great-grandparents were originally settled in...

item.title: These for-profit local news sites have become the “papers” of record in their communities
item.description: In most American cities, the largest local newsroom still belongs to the local newspaper. But that certainly doesn’t mean the largest local news audience always goes to the people with a printing press. And in some cases, the local traffic champ isn’t even a TV station or public radio station — it’s a for-profit, digital-native...
item.source:markdown: In most American cities, the largest local newsroom still belongs to the local newspaper. But that certainly doesn’t mean the largest local news audience always goes to the people with a printing press. And in some cases, the local traffic champ isn’t even a TV station or public radio station — it’s a for-profit, digital-native...

item.title: Minnesota — and MPR — kept everyone’s attention at the start of 2026
item.description: Minnesota Public Radio’s coverage of ICE put it at the top of the traffic rankings for public media websites in the last quarter of 2025. That trend continued in the first quarter of 2026; according to our regular rankings derived from Similarweb data, MPR saw 7 million more visits in January than in December, driven in...
item.source:markdown: Minnesota Public Radio’s coverage of ICE put it at the top of the traffic rankings for public media websites in the last quarter of 2025. That trend continued in the first quarter of 2026; according to our regular rankings derived from Similarweb data, MPR saw 7 million more visits in January than in December, driven in...

item.title: #1 Istanbul Vita – The best hair transplant Clinic in 2026

item.title: “Affiliation, not just access”: Newsrooms try to move beyond membership to a focus on “belonging”
item.description: Facing declining trust, unreliable social platforms, and search traffic weakened by AI summaries, the media industry is looking for a deeper way to hold onto audiences. Could the answer be a sense of “belonging”? “I think belonging is trying to figure out ways for the audiences to feel heard and seen, to engage or learn...
item.source:markdown: Facing declining trust, unreliable social platforms, and search traffic weakened by AI summaries, the media industry is looking for a deeper way to hold onto audiences. Could the answer be a sense of “belonging”? “I think belonging is trying to figure out ways for the audiences to feel heard and seen, to engage or learn...

item.title: The European Union backs Italy’s right to make Meta pay for news
item.description: Meta must comply with Italian law requiring it to negotiate with and fairly compensate news publishers for the the use of their content, according to a ruling by the Europe’s highest court on Tuesday. The decision by the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) found that EU copyright law doesn’t prohibit individual countries...
item.source:markdown: Meta must comply with Italian law requiring it to negotiate with and fairly compensate news publishers for the the use of their content, according to a ruling by the Europe’s highest court on Tuesday. The decision by the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) found that EU copyright law doesn’t prohibit individual countries...

item.title: In Atlanta, the push for digital subscriptions hasn’t taken off as much as hoped
item.description: For American newspapers, the past decade-plus has been all about seeking digital subscribers. The print business is shot. Digital advertising is shot. And while not everyone can be The New York Times — now the proud provider of 13 million subscriptions — even most local and regional newspapers have made digital subs Priority No. 1....
item.source:markdown: For American newspapers, the past decade-plus has been all about seeking digital subscribers. The print business is shot. Digital advertising is shot. And while not everyone can be The New York Times — now the proud provider of 13 million subscriptions — even most local and regional newspapers have made digital subs Priority No. 1....

item.title: Behind the scenes at Wirecutter for an epic duel of air purifiers
item.description: Tim Heffernan lights five matches with a single stroke and waves them up and down to spread the smoke. He sets them on a dinner plate as the small conference room fills with the smell of burning wood. He checks his particle meter to make sure the room is sufficiently smoky, then steps around the...
item.source:markdown: Tim Heffernan lights five matches with a single stroke and waves them up and down to spread the smoke. He sets them on a dinner plate as the small conference room fills with the smell of burning wood. He checks his particle meter to make sure the room is sufficiently smoky, then steps around the...

item.title: Trolling, memes, and deepfakes: How AI is thickening the fog of war
item.description: War has never been fought only on the ground. Clausewitz’s concept of the “fog of war” once described the uncertainty and confusion that cloud battlefield decision-making. Errol Morris’ 2003 documentary made the phrase a shorthand for the moral and informational ambiguities of modern conflict. But in the digital age, where war is also filmed, edited and promoted...
item.source:markdown: War has never been fought only on the ground. Clausewitz’s concept of the “fog of war” once described the uncertainty and confusion that cloud battlefield decision-making. Errol Morris’ 2003 documentary made the phrase a shorthand for the moral and informational ambiguities of modern conflict. But in the digital age, where war is also filmed, edited and promoted...

item.title: ‘NELA’ offers a glimmer of hope with food on people’s tables

item.title: César Chávez is being reexamined, where does that leave Chicano Studies?

item.title: BREAKING: Canvas hacked ahead of finals week

item.title: Semafor’s new AI tool helped boil down its entire flagship conference into nine takeaways
item.description: On April 13, more than 500 CEOs and other power brokers gathered in Washington, D.C. to join Semafor World Economy. Across the five-day event, hundreds of speakers took the stage, including Goldman Sachs President John Waldron, Singapore’s Deputy Prime Minister Gan Kim Yong, and nine sitting U.S. cabinet officials. In the weeks since the event...
item.source:markdown: On April 13, more than 500 CEOs and other power brokers gathered in Washington, D.C. to join Semafor World Economy. Across the five-day event, hundreds of speakers took the stage, including Goldman Sachs President John Waldron, Singapore’s Deputy Prime Minister Gan Kim Yong, and nine sitting U.S. cabinet officials. In the weeks since the event...

item.title: News podcasts are, increasingly, something you watch (but The Daily still works best as audio)
item.description: Large publishers see video as one big future for podcasts, according to a report out Thursday from the Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism (RISJ). They’re certainly not abandoning audio, but they’re aware that many young people hear “podcast” and think “YouTube.” “The discovery mechanisms for video are much better. Video as a medium...
item.source:markdown: Large publishers see video as one big future for podcasts, according to a report out Thursday from the Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism (RISJ). They’re certainly not abandoning audio, but they’re aware that many young people hear “podcast” and think “YouTube.” “The discovery mechanisms for video are much better. Video as a medium...

item.title: Google highlights links from subscribed publications in new AI Overviews update
item.description: When a Google search user encounters an AI Overview or an AI Mode response, the response will now highlight whether it includes information that comes from a publication the user subscribes to. Google claims that in early testing, people were “significantly more likely” to click through to a webpage that had this “Subscribed” label. In...
item.source:markdown: When a Google search user encounters an AI Overview or an AI Mode response, the response will now highlight whether it includes information that comes from a publication the user subscribes to. Google claims that in early testing, people were “significantly more likely” to click through to a webpage that had this “Subscribed” label. In...

item.title: Australia’s building a great system to fund local journalism — but it doesn’t want to use it
item.description: In 2022, I got cranky with the nation of Australia over its News Media Bargaining Code — its convoluted scheme to get money out of big tech companies’ pockets and into news companies’. The idea in a nutshell was this: Two giant American tech companies, Google and Meta, had abused Australian news publishers by “taking”...
item.source:markdown: In 2022, I got cranky with the nation of Australia over its News Media Bargaining Code — its convoluted scheme to get money out of big tech companies’ pockets and into news companies’. The idea in a nutshell was this: Two giant American tech companies, Google and Meta, had abused Australian news publishers by “taking”...

item.title: ProPublica gets a new look built to work across platforms
item.description: On Tuesday, ProPublica rolled out a redesign that revamped its homepage and aims to make its work “more recognizable and distinct” across platforms from Instagram to Apple News. The redesign goes beyond updated logos and typefaces; some of the changes are structural as well as aesthetic, geared toward showing audiences all the work that goes...
item.source:markdown: On Tuesday, ProPublica rolled out a redesign that revamped its homepage and aims to make its work “more recognizable and distinct” across platforms from Instagram to Apple News. The redesign goes beyond updated logos and typefaces; some of the changes are structural as well as aesthetic, geared toward showing audiences all the work that goes...

item.title: The Intercept didn’t just publish a story about ICE — it drove it around JFK
item.description: When the Trump administration sent Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents to American airports in March, The Intercept published a tip sheet for travelers, “How to keep ICE agents out of your phone at the airport.” The piece, by security researcher Nikita Mazurov, pulled in more than 150,000 pageviews, according to Intercept CEO Annie Chabel,...
item.source:markdown: When the Trump administration sent Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents to American airports in March, The Intercept published a tip sheet for travelers, “How to keep ICE agents out of your phone at the airport.” The piece, by security researcher Nikita Mazurov, pulled in more than 150,000 pageviews, according to Intercept CEO Annie Chabel,...

item.title: Newsletters, live coverage, a one-time magazine: The World Cup is becoming a testbed for journalism experiments
item.description: The FIFA World Cup, which begins on June 11, is by many measures the biggest World Cup in history: 48 teams will compete in 104 matches across the United States, Canada, and Mexico; ticket and transit prices are through the roof; and taxpayers have shelled out hundreds of millions of dollars to keep up with...
item.source:markdown: The FIFA World Cup, which begins on June 11, is by many measures the biggest World Cup in history: 48 teams will compete in 104 matches across the United States, Canada, and Mexico; ticket and transit prices are through the roof; and taxpayers have shelled out hundreds of millions of dollars to keep up with...

item.title: People are stressed out by most news that isn’t local news, according to a new study
item.description: The way people of different age groups in the United States get their news is different, but no matter how old they are, everyone is pretty stressed about all news that isn’t local news. That’s according to a new study from the Media Insights Project, a collaboration of The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs...
item.source:markdown: The way people of different age groups in the United States get their news is different, but no matter how old they are, everyone is pretty stressed about all news that isn’t local news. That’s according to a new study from the Media Insights Project, a collaboration of The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs...
